EVENT GENERAL
INTRODUCTION
This Event Management Plan (EMP) establishes the details for a street spectacle titled
The Whale Street to be held on Friday 17th Saturday 18th July 2026 and is a key document
in the planning and management of the event. It should be used as a reference tool for
all stakeholders, statutory agencies and is circulated to all parties in advance for
clarification and agreement.
This EMP is subject to amendments and updates during the planning of the event, up to
and including event day, although the latter should be minor. Where possible all changes
will be documented and circulated to all however all stakeholders should note the REV
version of the EMP for the most up to date version of the EMP.
REVISION
This is a REVISION of the Event Management Plan for GIAF The Whale Streetstreet
spectacle
See Previous versions below:
REVISION DATE AUTHOR
Draft 16th April 2026 Frank Commins

APPOINTED ROLE
For this event, Adam Fitzsimons is fulfilling the role of event organiser on behalf of Galway
International Arts Festival and will provide the Event Manager at the event.

COMPONENTS OF THE PLAN
This EMP has been prepared in accordance with appropriate Legislation, Regulations,
Guidelines, Codes of Practices and Bye Laws and includes the following fundamental
components:
● Event Information
● Medical and stewarding information
● Emergency and contingency plans
● Crowd safety and management information
SENSITIVE INFORMATION
Event arrangements may involve sensitive and/or confidential information, and as such
will not be disclosed to unauthorised persons or the general public. Where deemed
necessary certain information has been omitted from this EMP, for safety and security
reasons, however all key personnel and necessary persons will be briefed in person.
CONSULTATIONS
Throughout the planning of this event consultations are ongoing between Galway City
Council and Adam Fitzsimons and Event Control and the following:
● Galway City Council
● Ań Garda Síochána
● Civil Defence
● Fire Services
● HSE Emergency Planning
MEETINGS
All planning for this event is managed by Adam Fitzsimons in addition to ongoing
planning, other meetings and site visits will occur in the planning of this event. Site visits
still need to take place to ensure adequate planning takes place.
EVENT MANAGEMENT
There will be a full Event Management team in place for this event. Key roles have been
identified and key personnel have been appointed to those roles. All key personnel are
experienced in their respective roles and will be supported in the performance of their
duties and responsibilities by their colleagues on the Event Management Team.
The Event Management Team will coordinate all event arrangements in consultation with
Steward at the event. They will work closely with volunteers, stewards and An Garda
Síochána at the event. The Event Management Team will oversee the organising of
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 2of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
suitable stewards and security, together with all other necessary personnel, on duties
both in and around the event space. The Event Management Team will ensure that an
adequate number of staff is deployed for the event.
The Event Management Team will be in overall control of the event unless a Major
Emergency is declared (as per the Framework of Emergency Management definition), at
which time, the most applicable primary response agency (PRA), (An Garda Síochána,
HSE or Local Authority) will take control of the emergency. In this case the Event
Management Team will put at the disposal of the Emergency Controller all resources
present and available to aid in dealing with the emergency. See further details in the
Emergency Procedures section below.
All personnel will be given a briefing in advance of the event outlining their role, duties
and responsibilities. It is then the responsibility of key Event Management Personnel to
brief their respective teams.
EVENT DETAILS
Event: The Whale Street
Dates: Friday 17th and Saturday 18th 2026.
Start Time: Friday @21.30 and Saturday @ 21:30
Finish Times:Friday @22.45 and Saturday @ 22:45
Estimated Duration: 75 minutes.
Projected Audience: 10,000 people
1.1 The Event Route is as follows:
The company will have set up base at Nimmo’sPier for assembly and dressing
room.They will travel from there under escort along Merchants Rd to arrive in Eyre
Square to the entrance of Eyre Square shopping centre.They leave Eyre Square on to
Shop street ,Mainguard Street, Bridge Street,Dominick Street ,and finish outside
Monroe’s and Fairhill Rd Lower turning left onto Fr Griffin Road and finishing at a
performance space in Spanish Arch.

ATTENDEE PROFILE
The attendees will be mixed, with families and individuals.
EXPECTED WEATHER
This event will be subject to the weather conditions on the evening. Depending on the
conditions, the event procession will respond accordingly.
In the event of inclement weather,following consultations with Met Eireann the event will
not take place.
Weather conditions include wind monitoring with hand held anemometer will be closely
monitored before and during the event.
STEWARDING
The event stewarding and security for the event will be provided by C2 Security . All
personnel will receive full and thorough briefing on event days.
TRAFFIC MANAGEMENT
A taffic management plan will be required for this event.Galway City Council have agreed
to support and supply vehicles to satisfy the Hostile Vehicles Mitigation(HVM) aspect of
the plan,it is proposed that these vehicles be used to close the following roads.
These vehicles will be attended at all times and can be moved quickly upper request from
Contol room in the event of emergency vehicles movement.
The proposal is that vehicles will be deployed at the below locations
Vehicle blocks:
• At Eyre Square (Eyre Square Shopping Centre), the taxi rank, and the top
of Eyre Square East/Prospect Hill.
• Pedestrian Area covered by existing vehicle mitigation barriers, this will
be lowered as the parade approaches and raised after it passes.
• At Eglinton Street at the intersection with Woodquay & Mary Street
and Market Street.
• The corner of Mill St and Dominick St
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
• At St Patrick’s School to prevent vehicles entering from one way Street.
• At the junction at Father Griffin Road and Fairhill Rd Upper.
• At the Dail Bar closing of entrance to Cross Street.
• Exit from Raven Terrace carpark onto Fairhill Rd Upper.
• Leonardo’s Car Park rear exit we will be requested to close for the
duration.
• Signage will be placed on streets seven days prior to event to inform
drivers of no parking during event times.It will be assess if vehicles need
to be removed on the day.
• Fr Griffin Road at Tesco.
• The old Palas cinema and junction of Merchants Rd lower.
There is a total of eleven locations requiring HVM closures.
Additional Measures
Dominck Street Upper will be closed for outdoor dining which we will commence
at an earlier time of 3pm on the Saturday.
Ravens Terrace will be closed for outdoor dining which we will commence at an
earlier time of 3pm on the Saturday.
This series of road closures will be put into place prior and during the event at timing
deemed necessary by event control and Ań Gardaí, the purpose of this is for the safety
and enjoyment for the public and participants.

MANAGEMENT STRUCTURE
Event Controller
The Event Controller has overall control and responsibility of the event on event day,
with the exception of during an emergency whereby the Emergency Controller may
assume authority. This person should be of suitable competence and authority to
manage the overall event and deal with all stakeholders involved. There may be a
committee or group actually organising all the details of the event however it is the
Event Controller that has ultimate primacy on all arrangements, and it is imperative that
this person has the ability to carry out this role suitably. The main duties of the Event
Controller include (this list is non-exhaustive);
i. having overall responsibility for the management of the event;
ii. being involved in and contributing to the planning meetings with the relevant
authorities i.e.: An Garda Siochana, HSE, Local Authority etc.;
ili. ensuring the provision of adequate personnel for the event;
iv. remaining contactable, in relation to the event, before, during and after the event
v. contributing to a post event meeting on the event.
It is important to note that this is not an honorary role,on the contrary, they have clear
legal and moral responsibilities, and they are essentially accountable for the safe
management of the event and the safety of all those involved. It is imperative that the
persons appointed to these roles are competent for these roles specifically and
if/where necessary external advice should be sought. It is also essential that these
persons are adequately insured for the role which they carry out. Competency is
defined as having the appropriate experience and knowledge appropriate to the nature
of the work to be undertaken.
SAFETY OFFICER

The Safety Officer has specific responsibility for the safety arrangements for the event.
They will be involved during the planning phase for the event and will advise on all safety
elements.
The appointed Safety Officer will be capable, competent and insured to perform their
duties. They will work closely with the Event Controller to ensure that safety is a
consideration in all-key decisions.
Specific responsibilities of the Safety Officer include, but are not limited to, the following:
● Fully comprehend the Event Management Plan
● Fully comprehend all relevant and impacting Codes of Practice, Regulations,
Legislation, Guidance and best practice
● Ensuring that all safety arrangements, as per Event Management Plan and any
relevant agreements are implemented as appropriate
● Preparing and delivering a structured and comprehensive briefing for relevant
staff and stakeholders
● Maintain a monitoring brief during the event
● Monitoring crowd movements and build-ups
● Implementing any remedial actions in order to relieve or avoid undesirable
issues during the event
● Ensuring effective reporting and recording arrangements are in place and
communicated to staff in advance
● Assisting in an emergency situation as required
● Undertaking any reasonable duties as necessary to ensure the smooth
running of the event
● Contributing to any and all Post Event Reviews and Post Event Reports with a
particular focus on safety elements
MEDICAL COORDINATOR
The Medical Coordinator has overall responsibility for the medical provision and medical
response to incidents on-site during the event. In relation to matters relating specifically
to the medical well-being and safety of all at the event, the Medical Coordinator will work
closely with the Event Management Team.
The appointed Medical Coordinator will be capable, competent and insured to perform
their duties. They will have extensive experience as a medical professional and in
positions of leadership within the medical environment and will bring to bear that
experience at all times during this event.
Specific responsibilities of members of the Medical Coordinator include, but are not
limited to, the following:

CHIEF STEWARD
The Chief Steward will be responsible for:
● Overall stewarding and security arrangements at the event
● Be competent to recognise critical crowd conditions, signs of crowd distress
and crowd dynamics.
● Co-ordinating the duties of all stewards and security in conjunction with the
Event Management Team.
● Preparing a Stewarding/Security plan, which will include, Personnel Details
and Licences (where applicable) and deployment details.
● Ensuring that all stewards and security sign in on the morning of the event and
details of same are returned to Event Control
● Ensuring that all stewards are adequately briefed and knowledgeable of all
arrangements in advance of event going live
● Deploying stewards and security as per plan and as necessary to deal with
situations as they arise.

● Know the layout of the location, where facilities are located and being fully

STEWARDS
Stewards will be responsible for:
● Ensuring that the public are safely accommodated within the viewing /
circulation areas in a planned manner, so as to ensure the safety and comfort
of all spectators at the event.
● Be aware of the Medical and Fire Safety arrangements.
● Be familiar with the Emergency Plan, Procedures and their part in its operation
and specified duties to be undertaken
● Give immediate access to Gardaí and other emergency services in the event
of an emergency
● Control and / or direct the public who are entering or leaving the area and help
achieve an even flow of people as directed
● Be competent to recognise crowd densities, signs of crowd distress and
crowd dynamics so as to help ensure safe dispersal of the public and ensure
there is no overcrowding
● Be courteous and provide information to the public and give assistance if
required
● Exercise proper management over the public
● When requested, assist at barriers and giving information to attendees
approaching the event
● Ensure that emergency access routes and emergency egress routes are kept
clear and free from obstruction at all times
● Prevent any climbing on fences and other structures; if in difficulty they should
immediately contact their Chief Steward or a Garda
● Assist Gardaí with crowd management as requested
● Investigate promptly any disturbance or other reported incident
● Report immediately to Chief Steward or Garda, if any fire or other emergency
comes to notice.

SAFETY & OPERATIONS
EVENT SPECIFIC SAFETY STRATEGY
SAFETY POLICY
It is the policy of GIAF to provide a safe environment for this event. It will be achieved by
ensuring that all facets of the event are managed in a manner so as to maximise the
safety of all those attending and participating, together with the provision of proper safety
management systems for all elements of the event. This will be achieved in cooperation
with the Event Management team, Chief Steward, An Garda Síochána, Medical,
Volunteers as well as all suppliers and stakeholders.
Throughout the planning process Galway International Arts Festival will try to ensure that
the attendees have an enjoyable experience in a safe environment. There are many
components to this and these are detailed below.
RISK ASSESSMENT
RISK ASSESSMENT
The risk assessments for this event have been developed with the following in mind:
● Hazards may change during the course of the event.
● Risk levels can be dynamic. This necessitates on-going dynamic risk
assessment on the part of the Event Management Team during the event.
● The Likelihood and Consequence relating to specific, identified hazards vary
depending, predominantly, on where the hazard occurs, what level of risk is
apparent and what level of control is required.
The comprehensive risk assessment process undertaken for this event includes the
following:
● Identification of foreseeable hazards.
● Establish level of risk of the hazard causing harm or damage
● Establish the likelihood and consequences of those hazards.
● Determine the controls required to eliminate or reduce the level of risk, using
the hierarchy of controls.
Consideration is given to design, information and management of the event elements to
try to eliminate or at least minimise the risk. The findings of the risk assessment will be
continuously updated and documented on the worksheets.
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 13of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
It is noted that the risk assessment undertaken in connection with this event
management plan does take into consideration the hazards and risks from structural
elements in the event space, however the Event Management team cannot take
responsibility for these hazards and risks that they cannot control, i.e.
● Pavements and kerbs in situ
● Road surfaces
● Underfoot ground conditions
● Permanent walls in situ
● Permanent or non-event fencing and railings
● Street furniture
● Risks of building elements on the public
● Premises/ buildings/sites adjacent to the event space
● Fire/explosion/toxic risks from such premises/sites.
● Terrorist activity
● Bicycles racks and bicycle hire stations
Where required, and safely accessible, the hazard will be confined and accessed
restricted.
The event risk assessment for this event is included as an attachment.
BUILD, BREAK, TEMPORARY STRUCTURES
EVENT LAYOUT
Full drawings for the event are included in appendix.
SITE CONSERVATION
All buildings and associated site-works to be always protected.
TEMPORARY BARRIERS
There will be a small number of crowd control barriers (CCB’s) used as part of the Safety
Management for the event. GIAF are the appointed supplier and as such, they will be
erected and dismantled by experienced staff.
BUILD
A comprehensive build and production schedule is being developed and is continuously
being updated following communications between the event managers and relevant
stakeholders.
The build elements of this event will commence on Thursday 16th July 2026. This will take
place at Nimmo’s Pier where a base will be set up while the company are in Galway.

The company have supplied a specific Risk Assessment and Method statement (RAMS)
will also require sign off before commencement of build.
Any changes on site will need to be amended on the RAMS prior to works continuing.
The Safety Officer who will ensure adequate measures are taken to ensure people’s
safety will supervise relevant elements of the build.
BREAK
The break elements of this event will take place immediately after the event in the docks,
The Whale Street will be escorted from Spanish Arch after the crowds have moved on.
DAMAGE TO PROPERTY
No element of the event should specifically give rise to damage being caused to any
property in and around the event site.
Stewarding and event safety staff will be briefed to maintain a monitoring role in relation
to it and to intervene if any damage is seen to be done.

CROWD SAFETY AND MOVEMENT
EXPECTED NUMBERS
This is a free event. The numbers expected to attend will be approximately 10,000, based
on previous year attendance but also very weather dependent if a wet day the numbers
will be less.
CAPACITY CALCULATIONS
A review of the event-specific capacity calculations will be carried out and will continue
to be reviewed as any amendments or additions occur. These calculations consider all
the relevant physical (P) and safety (S) factors, and include:
● Ingress Capacity which relates to the time required for the maximum number
of attendees to enter the event through the entry points
● Occupant capacity, which relates to the maximum number of occupants
(participants, spectators and personnel), that area can comfortably and safely
maintain.
● Circulation space, which is the space available for the participants,
spectators and personnel to move around. When calculating, consideration
has been given to items such as fixed structures in situ and space given to
event elements for example barriers and sterile area, as well as exit routes.
● Egress Capacity which relates to the time required for the maximum number
of attendees to exit the event through the exit points.
● Emergency egress that is concerned with the egress capacity in the case of an
emergency and ensuring there are sufficient routes for the expected
occupants (participants, spectators and personnel).
When reviewing the capacities for the event, various areas and matters are considered
and these included:
● Main Entry/Exits
● Emergency routes
It has been established that the venue is a suitable site for the proposed activity. The
arrangements are carefully planned to ensure the comfort and safety of the attendees as
well as the spectators and personnel working throughout the overall event, and have
been developed by the event management team to ensure all have adequate capacity,
and these include:
● Clear access routes
● Location and positioning of crowd control barriers
● Location and positioning of stewards and security
● Location and positioning of event related structures
● Occupant and egress routes of event spaces calculated

This plan is designed in reference to previous Parades and public spectacles including

Eyre Square Crowd Management Plan.
The float and performers will set up on the pedestrian area near the public toilets
following a escorted trip from the docks A compound of crowd control barriers will be
set up to have the space set aside.There will be no delay once they reach this point for
the spectator to begin.Aside from the traffic management plan the pedestrian numbers
and audience will need to be monitored at this point as the festival garden will be in
operation.Concerns regarding the Corbettcourt shopping centre exit are been
addressed.
Staffing
Area manager and security will monitor and move crowds further on the route to view
the event. The Whale Street will have 6 security personnel around the structure who
purpose is to keep the public clear as it move thought the street. They will be assisted
by volunteers and Festival staff using rope to create a cordon around Micrococmos.
Following the closure of the road and the event begins and moves on to Williams Street,
this street is sufficiently wide enough to deal with expected crowds. Barriers will be
stored, and security will be stationed at Abbeygate Streets upper and lower to ensure
these streets remain clear and be used for emergency egress. Moving on to Shop Street,
barriers and security will be deployed to Church Lane and Buttermilk Lane and ensure
they are exit only if needed.
Security will all be need on both ends of Church yard Street as this is the wides and
main emergency exit from Shop street. Historically this location has had the greatest
crowd density as this location is been fed by High Street and Middle Street.
Security will need to be deployed to these locations if the need to divert pedestrians
away from Shop Street.
The performance moves on to Mainguard Street where some street furniture will need
to be remove from outside premises.The operations of the bike hire will need to be
contacted to close on the hire station and remove bikes to prevent damage and a trip
hazard for the public. The other bicycle rack will need to be closed also and signage put
up a week in advance to ask public not to use.

Security will need to be deployed on Cross Street upper and Cross Street lower at the
junction of Middle Street again to manage crowds movement and main the street as a
emergency route from the performance route.
The performance continues Bridge Street over O Brien’s Bridge. The path running also
side the river which exit on Fr Griffin Road will also require security and need to be
closed off to stop people taken a short cut to Fr Griffin Road and prevent congestion.
The performance will move on to Dominick Street where some restrictions will need to
be put in place for cars parked on this street to maintain a safe distance for the
performance to pass by the viewing public.
The final leg of the route is to finish outside Monroes and move back to Nimmo’s Pier.
Emergency Plan
The spectacle has one float and 6 participants
In the event of emergency vehicles approaching the junction from either direction,
Security Staff stationed at other points along the route will radio to Security Staff
positioned at the junction, who will direct that The Whale Streetto move to the side of
the road until the emergency vehicles have safely transited the junction.
EMERGENCY ACCESS
Access for emergency services will be maintained at all time.
CIRCULATION AND EVACUATION ROUTES
All circulation and evacuation routes will have adequate area to allow for the expected
occupancy. Attendees can circulate through the spaces with little or no restrictions
however subject to crowd congestion in various locations.
The site is a partially open space. The expected total audience is approximately 10000
people throughout the event. There will be no fenced areas and escape will be available
in all directions.
EXITS AND EMERGENCY EXITS
Attendees will be directed through exit routes, referred to Stewards, who will be present
to monitor, regulate and manage the crowd, and will supervise all exit routes.
During an emergency the crowd will exit through the nearest exit / route available, and
this may be to a place of reasonable safety initially however will lead to an evacuation
route.

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
SECURITY / STEWARDS
CONSULTATION
Stewarding/Security requirements for this event have been confirmed after
consultations between the Event Management Team, the Chief Steward and An Garda
Síochána. Relevant experience, expertise, site visits and several meetings have informed
these requirements.
STAFFING
This event has been split into a number of component parts for the stewarding plan. This
is based on a number of factors, including:
● Event site layout
● Crowd profile Event Timing
● Expected attendance
● Entry / Exit widths
● Consultation with An Garda Síochána
LOCATIONS
The Chief Steward will ensure the following locations are covered at all times as per plan.
Periodic checks will be completed of these positions to confirm cover:
● Entrance Points
● Exits Points
● Appropriate location as designated
● Carparks
REDEPLOYMENT
Stewards, security and/or volunteers may be redeployed where required providing that it
does not comprise the stewarding of other areas. Any redeployment will be agreed in
advance between Chief Steward and the Event Management Team.
BRIEFINGS
It is the responsibility of the Chief Steward to ensure cascade briefings are delivered
ensuring that all Stewards are familiar with their responsibilities and duties.
Senior Stewards are required to ensure that all stewards sign in and provide contact
details, as well as ensuring they all have necessary contact details and incident report
forms to be completed. Any accident/incidents that occur will be fully documented.
Report will be issued to GIAF for record purposes.

All Senior Stewards will be issued with an instructional pack, which will include the
following,
● Details of duties required from stewards
● Contact details for key personnel
● Site Map where relevant
MEDICAL PROVISIONS
All medical arrangements will be coordinated by Galway Civil Defence.A full medical
plan is attached in the Appendix.
FIRE SAFETY
All reasonable fire safety precautions will be taken during this event.
Fire certificates for materials used in floats and temporary structures to be furnished to
parade coordinators.
There are some fireworks in this year display and a more detail plan of these to be
furnished in a revised plan.
CAPACITES/EGRESS/INGRESS
Safe holding capacities for the site been calculated as per Section Crowd Safety &
Movement.
To ensure safe exiting of attendees in the case of an emergency, the Safety Officer will
ensure all Exit Signage and Communication methods are in place and free from
obstruction prior to the event going live.
EQUIPMENT STANDARDS
The promoter will consult with a professional fire safety company to organise for
adequate fire equipment.
Once sited the fire safety company will provide on-site certification to confirm all
equipment has been tested within the last 11 months and is fit for purpose.

INFRASTRUCTURE AND INSTALLATIONS
Certification will be requested for all materials, structural linings, bespoke installations
and any material deemed relevant.
GENERAL AND COMBUSTIBLE WASTE
Combustible waste and general waste produced as part of this event will be removed as
and when required to prevent any build up. This will be the responsibility of the Galway
City Council and will be monitored by the Event Management Team.
SMOKING
Under the Public Health (Tobacco) Acts, it has been illegal to smoke in all enclosed
workplaces. All Staff working on site will be briefed and this will be monitored on site by
all management staff.
Smoking is not permitted in the vicinity of an of the event installations.
ELECTRICAL INSTALLATION & LIGHTING
All lights used in the event are supplied and certified before use. All emergency lighting
in the tent will be tested and reci certified All tower lighting for outside use for walkways
and exits will be also certified and tested.
WASTE MANAGEMENT
It is the policy of promoter to ensure that there is no build-up of combustible within or as
a result of this event. At the time of drawing up the Draft there was no waste been
generated by this event.
WASTE STORAGE AND REMOVAL
A post de-rig clean will occur after the build site has been cleared to ensure it is restored
to pre-event state.
ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION
NOISE
All noise will be for a limited period and shall not cause any unreasonable disturbance to
any nearby noise sensitive premises.

TOILETS FACILITIES
Toilets will be made available to staff and participants at Nimmo’s Pier at the beginning
and end of the parade.
TRAFFIC MANAGEMENT
CONSULTATION
The traffic management arrangements for this event will be established in consultation
with An Garda Síochána and Galway City Council. Consideration is given at all times to
the impact on general public and all public transport and private providers.
ROAD CLOSURES
There are a series of road closures required for this event. These will be decided on by
Garda management and event control depending on the factors occurring at the time.
PARKING
There is parking available throughout the city.

There will be parking restrictions on Dominick St and the parking spaces the end of Bridge
Street at the Bridge Mills, barriers will be put in place early morning and signage to be
erected in this locationt least 7 days in advance of the spectacle. Any vehicle causing an
obstruction will be dealt with in accordance with the road traffic act.
A management plan will need to drawn up for these spaces as there is 2 spectator event.
EMERGENCY ACCESS
Emergency access, if required, will be facilitated as a priority through the nearest access
point.
DELIVERIES TO SITE
All deliveries of the float to the docks in the days before the event.

POLICIES & PROCEDURES
EVENT CONTROL & COMMUNICATIONS
EVENT CONTROL
Event Control will be located upstairs in the Galway Advertiser Offices Eyre Square.
The Event Management Team of four area managers will manage all actions and will have
clear communications with working personnel, stewards and An Garda Síochána
members.
In the case of a serious or potential Major Emergency the Emergency Team will assume
control, which consists of:
● Event Management Team
● Chief Steward
● An Garda Síochána
The Event Management Team will make all necessary decisions until such a time that a
Transfer of Primacy is required to the applicable statutory agency.
COMMUNICATIONS
Communication between the Event Management Team and those working on the event
is a key component to the Safety Management of the event. The main communications
method for this event will be digital two-way radios.
Radios will be allocated to ensure the efficient and effective safe management of the
event.
During the event, there will be multiple methods of communicating with the participants
and spectators.
They include the following:
● Megaphones will be utilised if required.
● Stewards.
● On the ground event teams.
KEY CONTACTS
The Key Contacts list will be circulated among key members of staff.

MEGAPHONES
There will be megaphones available for use as required and security will be briefed on
pro-active communications with attendees.
LOST AND FOUND
Any items found should be handed into event representatives and logged if possible. At
the end of the event lost or found items will be passed to An Garda Síochána.
LOST PERSON
The lost person policy for this event is as follows:
Finds a lost person
● If a team member finds a lost person, they relay this to the Event Management
Team using the designated code word “DELTA 1”, along with any pertinent
information in relation to the lost person.
● The team member will remain in situ with the lost person for 10-15 minutes or as
directed by the Event Management Team. A nearby team member or volunteer
may be directed to that area to accompany the team member.
● Depending on the location the team member, along with another team member /
volunteer, may be directed to an accessible and easily visible area
● The Children First: National Guidance for the Protection and Welfare of Children,
updated Children First Act 2015 will be adhered to with young persons protection
to the foremost.
● From when the Event Management Team is informed of the lost person efforts will
be made to locate those who were accompanying the lost person.
● the Event Management Team will evaluate each incident individually and decide
on the best course of action. This may be to remain in that area.
● Any accident/incidents that occur will be fully documented.
● the Event Management Team will communicate all information to the other
agencies.
Report of a lost person,
● If a team member receives a report of a lost person, they relay this to the Event
Management Team, using the designated code word “ROGUE 1”, along with key
details to include the lost person’s name, age and description and where they
were last seen.
● The team member will stay with the reporting person if possible or alternatively
obtain a contact name and number at least.

Incident Management Plan:
Incident: Emergency vehicle needs to access to parade route
The event controller on being informed of the incident will immediately replay the
following message to all personnel:
“AN EMERGENCY SERVICE VEHICLE NEEDS TO GAIN ACCESS TO THE PARADE ROUTE
FROM (INSERT STREET NAME), PLEASE MOVE ALL PARTICIPANTS IN YOUR SECTION TO
THE RIGHT HAND SIDE OF THE DIRECTION OF THE PARADE “In this incident all
participants are moved to the right hand side of the street to allow vehicle access on the
left hand side of the direction of the parade. The audience on the left hand side should
stay where they are, but stewards should ask them to move off the road to allow access.
A member of Stewarding Personnel will clear all access and egress routes. Two
Ambulances will be present on site at the event provided by the Civil Defense. Set
positions outlined in the Event plan.
INCIDENT: Fire or other incident in a building/facility adjacent to the parade.
The Garda office on being informed of the incident will immediately inform the Event
Controller by radio/phone of the nature and location of the incident who will ensure
emergency services are call out/proper action is taken. If the alarm is raised by a member
of the public/ others via a 999/ 112 call, the fire service regional control centre (WRCC)
will have standing instructions to immediately inform the Event controller. The Event
Controller will send a route map and detail access and egress routes in the parade. The
Event Controller will request the appropriate orders to Gardaí representative in the room
to Officers, Civil Defence and Stewards in the relevant areas, to evacuate the affected
part of the venue/route to ensure no further crowd flows into the area. The designated
steward will be required to make the correct emergency announcements.
Incident: Crowd Surge / Crushing / Congestion / Over Crowding in the Venue / Route

Any member of event control staff of the three principal response agencies will inform
the Event Controller.
The event controller will inform the Stewards in the relevant area to:
Implement the appropriate crowd diversion plan for that area. Once the access street
has been closed except to exiting audience, all crowds will be diverted to areas
specified by the controller. The designated area Head Steward will be instructed to
make the appropriate emergency evacuation announcements.
Incident: Person Falling In River
Preventative measures will be in place.These measures include a number of stewards
backed up with members of Claddagh Watch will be briefed accordingly where they will
advise the public not to sit on the bridge wall. The Fire Service will be called directly
through 999 should a person fall into the river.
Persons will be rescued in accordance with the standard emergency procedures for
water rescue.
Incident: Infiltration of route or Reviewing stand by Uninvited Groups
The Area supervisor or Area Garda officer will immediately inform the Event controller
identifying the extent and the location of the incident.
The event controller, Senior Garda office in charge will issue the appropriate orders to
Garda officers/stewards in the relevant area to:
Implement the appropriate crowd diversion plan for that area/Reviewing stand. Once
the access street/Reviewing stand has been closed except to exiting audience, all
crowds will be diverted to areas specified by the controller and senior Garda Officer.
The designated area Head Steward will be instructed to make the appropriate
emergency evacuation announcements.

Incidents
If you discover an incident immediately inform Event Control by one of the following
means:
● Radio (however in the event of suspect packages do not use your radio)
● Telephone Event Control
● Make your way to the Civil Defense Vehicle located in front of Bank of Ireland
Eyre Square
In relaying information about an incident do not use emotive words such as FIRE.
The descriptive words are:
● USA for Fire
● UKRAINE for Crowd Disturbance
When an incident has been discovered event control will issue a general broadcast via
the radio. The general broadcast will be as follows:
“GENERAL BROADCAST FROM EVENT CONTROL TO ALL UNITS. THERE IS A
(DESCRIPTION OF THE NATURE OF THE INCIDENT, E.G. USA IN THE (LOCATION E.G.
SHOP STREET)
This message will then be repeated.
After an assessment of the incident the event controller, safety officer and Gardaí will
agree on the actions to be deployed including consideration of the following:
● If the incident can be contained
● Whether to issue a standby or evacuation message
● Whether a part or full evacuation is necessity and the location of the evacuation
points

STAND BY
Event control will issue a standby message over the radio:
“WILL THE (REQUIRED PERSONNEL) PLEASE GO TO THE (SCENE OF THE INCIDENT)”
Event Control will wait for confirmation from all commands that instructions are
complete.
EVACUATION ACTIONS
All channels will be notified evacuation message with reference to the designated
exits/assembly points.
Where public address and megaphones are available event control/head stewards
issues the following public evacuation message:
‘LADIES AND GENTLEMEN DUE TO CIRCUMSTANCES THAT HAVE ARISEN IT WILL NOT
BE POSSIBLE TO CONTINUE TODAYS EVENT AND YOU ARE REQUIRED TO LEAVE THE
VIEWING AREA AS QUICKLY AND QUIETLY AS POSSIBLE FOLLOWING THE DIRECTION
OF STEWARDS.”
Monitor in consultation with others the safe evacuation of patrons/staff from the area.
POST EVACUATION PLANS
Receive acknowledgement from all commands that:
● Evacuation is compete
● All areas are clear
STAND DOWN ACTIONS
If evacuation is not required request event control issues the stand down message to
all commands.
“THE PREVIOUS MESSAGE FOR THE (STAFF MEMBER) IS CANCELLED”
This message should then be repeated.

Emergency plans
EMERGENCY CONTROLLER
The Emergency Controller, in the event of an emergency situation and following a
Transfer of Primacy from the Event Management Team, has specific responsibility for the
implementation of the Emergency Management Plan appropriate to the given situation.
The Emergency Controller may, as required, be one of the following:
● Senior member of An Garda Síochána
● Senior Fire Officer
● Senior Ambulance Officer
The plan of all Principal Response Agencies would be implemented in the event of a
declared Major Emergency.
The objective of the Emergency Plan is to outline the procedures to be adopted in the
event of an emergency being declared at the event, or somewhere with proximity such
that it has an impact on the event and the safety of those in attendance.
POTENTIAL EMERGENCY SITUATIONS
Emergency situations, by their nature, can take many forms and comprise many
elements. That said, there are some emergency situations, which have been known to
occur at events more than others. They include:
● Weather
● Environmental Issue
● Fire
● Crowd Disorder
● Medical / Health Emergency
● Structural Compromising / Collapse
● Explosion
● Bomb Threat
KEY COMMUNICATION POINTS
In the event of an emergency, the following are key communication points:
● Location of the actual emergency
● Staff on scene – numbers and competencies
● Assessment of casualties and risks
DEFINITIONS
A serious incident can be defined as ‘circumstance or potential circumstance, which
has caused or has potential to cause, injury to people or damage to property or the
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 1 of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
environment which can be managed by the resources immediately available on site such
as stewards/security, medical and/or Emergency Services’. Examples include:
● Outbreak of small fire
● Minor crowd disorder
● Medical incidents affecting an individual or small number of people
An emergency can be defined as ‘circumstance or potential circumstance, which has
caused or has potential to cause, serious injury to people or damage to property or the
environment requiring an immediate response including the deployment of resources
from the event management team and/or principal emergency services (An Garda
Síochána, Ambulance service, Fire service).’ The principal emergency services will be
requested to assist in the response and will work in conjunction with event management.
A major emergency can be defined as ‘circumstance or potential circumstance which
has caused or has potential to cause severe injury and/or loss of life and which is beyond
the capabilities of resources on site and/or additional available resources brought in to
assist’.
Under a Framework for Major Emergency Management a Major Emergency is defined as:
“Any event which, usually with little or no warning, causes or threatens death or injury,
serious disruption of essential services or damage to property, the environment or
infrastructure beyond the normal capabilities of the principle emergency services in the
area in which the event occurs, and requires the activation of specific additional
procedures and the mobilization of additional resources to ensure an effective,
coordinated response”.
Any accident/incidents that occur will be fully documented. Report will be issued to
Galway City Council for record purposes
ACTIVATION OF EMERGENCY PLAN
During all normal operations management and control of the event rests with the Event
Management Team
If a situation occurs which is beyond the normal capabilities of the Event Management
team to manage, after consultation with key personnel including:
● the Event Management Team
● Medical Coordinator
● Chief Steward
The Event Management Team will consult with Galway City Council and advise that a
Transfer of Primacy may be required. The Transfer of Primacy will state that when the
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 32of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
Emergency Plan is implemented due to an incident occurring, the event management
team will no longer manage the event.
The Event Controller will consult with the Principle Response Agency (PRA) advising them
of the situation, and the PRA’s will coordinate an effective response. The lead PRA
representative, if required, will then declare a major emergency.
In practical terms, a Major Emergency exists when it is apparent that the safety
management and medical management resources in place at the event are not sufficient
and external resources will be required.
TRANSFER OF PRIMACY
Transfer of responsibility from the Event Management Team to an Emergency Controller,
and transfer back, will be logged in the Event Log and will also involve the signing of the
Transfer of Primacy form. Attached as an Appendix.
EMERGENCY CONTROLLER
The Emergency Controller shall take control over the entire operation unless or until the
circumstances of the emergency dictate otherwise, i.e., the Senior Fire Officer present
would take a major fire in charge. In this event the Emergency Controller would be
responsible for keeping the emergency area clear.
LOCATION OF EMERGENCY
The location of the actual emergency must be clearly identified by the Emergency
Controller before deciding which options in the emergency plan are to be activated.
FACILITIES
The Event Management Team shall ensure that all facilities and staff required for the
emergency are made available.
DIVERSION
If the Emergency Controller considers that a full (or at least substantial) diversion is
required, he/she will inform the Event Management Team, who will assist with the
management of the diversion. This decision can only be taken on the occurrence of an
agreed major emergency. All patrons will be directed away from the emergency towards
the nearest emergency route, which may be manned.
EMERGENCY ACCESS ROUTE
All relevant emergency services will be made aware of the emergency routes in advance
of the event. This happens as part of the event management planning process. All
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
Local Authorities
The Local Authorities shall carry out all of its functions in accordance with the provisions
of the plan, as well as:
● Activation of the plan
● Maintenance of all services during the emergency
● Accommodation and welfare of evacuees and persons displaced by the
emergency
● Provision of food and rest facilities for personnel responding to the emergency
Galway Fire Service
Galway Fire service shall carry out all its functions in accordance with the provisions of
the plan, as well as:
● Activation of the plan
● Extinction of fires
● Containment, neutralisation and clearance of chemical spills and emissions
● Protection of rescue of persons and property
● Provision of access to the site of the emergency
● Forensic support for Gardaí
● Advise on evacuation
● Site clearance, demolition, clean-up operations, removal and disposal of debris
● Control and direction of activities of all agencies within the “Danger Area” at an
emergency
HSE
HSE shall carry out all of its functions in accordance with the provisions of the plan, as
well as:
● Declaration of a Major Emergency and notifying the other two relevant principal
response agencies.
● Activation of predetermined procedures/arrangements in accordance with its
Major Emergency Mobilisation Procedure.
● Acting as lead agency, where this is determined in accordance with Appendix F7
(of the Framework), and undertaking the specified coordination function;
o Provision of medical advice and assistance;
o Provision of medical aid to casualties at the site;
o Triage of casualties, and assigning them to hospitals for evacuation;
o Casualty evacuation and ambulance transport;
o Provision of hospital treatment;
o Provision of psycho-social support to persons affected by the emergency;
o Certification of the dead;
o Support for An Garda Síochána forensic work;
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
o Support for the Coroner’s role;
● Clinical decontamination and decontamination of contaminated persons on
arrival at hospital;
● Advising and assisting An Garda Síochána and Local Authorities on public health
issues arising;
● Exercising control of any voluntary or other service which it mobilises to the site;
● Monitoring and/or reporting on the impact in its functional area of any
emergency/crisis which falls within the ambit of a “National Emergency”, and
coordinating/undertaking any countermeasures in its functional area which are
required/recommended by an appropriate national body;
● Any other function, related to its normal functions, which is necessary for the
management of the emergency/crisis;
● Any function which the On-Site Co-ordinating Group requests it to perform; and,
● Maintaining essential health services during the Major Emergency
EMERGENCY PROCEDURES
RESTRICTING OPERATIONS
Every effort will be made to ensure that the event proceeds smoothly; it is imperative to
plan for all unforeseen circumstances in order to ensure public safety. It may be
necessary to stop, curtail or limit the event in the interest of safety. If an event has to be
stopped, curtailed or limited, it will only occur upon the direction of the Event
Management Team.
There are certain factors that will be considered when deciding to stop, curtail or limit
the event these are as follows:
● Advice from the Medical Coordinator
● Advice from the Chief Steward
● Advice from the Statutory Agencies
CONTINGENCY PLANS
In the planning for this event a range of eventualities have been considered, which may
come to pass. This is not an exhaustive list but is informed by experience of events and
eventualities.
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 36of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
INCLEMENT WEATHER
The event is outdoors and therefore exposed to all the weather elements. There are no
facilities specifically for shelter provided by the event organisers. Stewards will be
briefed to assist where possible and without endangering themselves.
PRE-EVENT
The event management will monitor the forecasts and communicate directly with Met
Eireann if required to establish the most accurate forecast, and to take guidance from
the advisory service available.
DURING THE EVENT
Weather conditions will be continuously monitored and noted. If adverse weather
conditions occur the Event Management Team, will decide if the event should be
temporarily postponed or cancelled.
In the event of extreme conditions, sudden heavy rain or wind etc. the following will
occur:
● the Event Management Team will communicate with the key personnel and advise
of actions
● Stewards and event team will direct attendees away from any temporary
infrastructure and advise to take shelter where possible
● Medical will review and communicate with NEOC if required
● Emergency announcements will be made to advise attendees to evacuate the
area as required.
● The Event Management Team will continue to monitor the conditions and when
safe to do so the Event Management Team will decide if the event can resume or
to cancel completely.
FAILURE OF EQUIPMENT
● The contractor will carry out pre-event inspections early to determine all
equipment is working correctly.(Floats and Vehicles ) Report to the Event
Management Team if attention is needed. The Event Management Team will
decide promptly to replace equipment if repairs are not successful.
● All applicable personnel will set up the crowd control barriers in advance of the
event to establish that all are sufficient to use with the general public. Take out of
use, any which may cause further hazards.
● The Safety Officer will visually inspect all temporary structures.

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
DELAYS
● The Event Management Team will try to ensure that there are no delays to the start
however will have Key Personnel briefed just in case.
● Stewards will be in fixed positions throughout the event space to monitor the
progress.
● The Event Management Team will communicate with the Senior Garda Síochána
present to establish assistance if required.
SHORTAGE OF PERSONNEL
● At sign in any shortages will be identified. If applicable the Event Controller will
organise for additional external personnel to be directed to the event and will
continue to trouble-shoot.
● Personnel will be redeployed if necessary
● There is a contingency of 10% with all personnel
CANCELLATION POLICY
PRE-EVENT
In case of cancellation the Event Management Team will inform all key personnel and
Statutory Agencies and the communication plan will be activated to contact external
parties. The Event Management Team will:
● Inform all key personnel of the cancellation who will in turn ensure that all
personnel, event stewards and all other relevant parties are briefed before
initiating cancellation
● If staff are on site already, send out a radio alert to all personnel that the event is
being postponed/ cancelled
● Assign some first aid personnel to the key areas
● Instruct the PA announcer and/or personnel with loud hailers to make the
following announcements informing any public who may be present:
o “Ladies and gentlemen, we regret that for reasons beyond our control,
today’s event has been postponed / cancelled. We apologise for any
inconvenience. Thank you for your support and patience”.
● Consult with the Event PR team regarding messages on social media.
● Monitor any areas where spectators may have gathered
● Provide information to Chief Steward relating to the event.
● Notify the Gardaí if any crowd disturbances arise
Event stewards will adopt the following procedures:

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
● Prohibit admittance to unauthorised locations
● Keep emergency routes clear
● Remain courteous to all attendees and provide information to them when
requested
DURING THE EVENT
In case of cancellation of performance, the Event Management Team will inform all key
personnel and Statutory Agencies and the communication plan will be activated to
contact external parties. The Event Management Team will:
● Inform all key personnel of the cancellation who will in turn ensure that all
personnel, event stewards and security are briefed before initiating cancellation
● Send out a radio alert to all personnel that the event is being postponed /
cancelled
● Assign some first aid personnel to the key areas
● Instruct the personnel with loud hailers to make the following announcements
informing any public who may be present:
o “Ladies and gentlemen, we regret that for reasons beyond our control,
today’s event has been postponed/cancelled. We apologise for any
inconvenience. Thank you for your support and patience”.
● Provide information to Chief Steward relating to the event
● Notify the Gardaí if any crowd disturbances arise.
● Consult with the Event PR Team regarding messages on social media.
All key personnel will supervise and assist:
● Attendees external to the event space on proposed ingress into the venue.
● Manage crowds to ensure a calm and control egress and avoid contra-flows
forming.
● Remain courteous to all and provide information to them when requested
● Keep emergency routes clear
EVENT HEALTH & SAFETY DOCUMENTATION & INSPECTIONS
TESTS AND INSPECTIONS
It is the responsibility of the Safety Officer to ensure that all appropriate tests and
inspections are carried out at all stages of the event.
PRE-EVENT
During the planning phase of the event the Safety Officer will:
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 39of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
● Ensure that all relevant Statutory Agencies and stakeholders are aware of and
expressly agree to everything in the event management plan
● Manage all contractors, ensuring adequate provisions, arrangements and
documentation are in place with regard to safety
● Ensure that any build elements are carried out safely and adequately, that all
safety documentation is provided and, where required.
PRE-SHOW
In advance of the event opening to the public, the Safety Officer will:
● Ensure sufficient numbers of personnel and stewards are on hand
● Ensure all medical and fire-fighting arrangements are in place
● Ensure all signage is in place
● Ensure all entry and exit routes are free from obstruction and trip hazards
● Ensure that all emergency exits and routes are fully operational
● Ensuring all public-address systems are functioning
● Ensure there is no build-up of combustible waste
● Ensure there is no hazardous waste materials on site
DURING
During the course of the event, the Safety Officer will:
● Monitor crowd movement
● Respond to any incidents or accidents
● Monitor the event site for any changes that may represent a safety issue
● Ensure the status of all elements remain the same.
● Ensure all staff members are playing their part in ensuring safety
EVENT END
As the event finishes, the Safety Officer will:
● Monitor egress, ensuring all spectators exit from the area safely.
● Ensure the de-rig / break down of any elements is done in a safe manner
● Ensure the removal of any combustible waste
● Ensure the event site is reinstated as per event management plan
STAFF HEALTH & SAFETY PROCEDURES
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
Under Section 8 of the Safety, Health and Welfare at Work Act 2005 the employer has a
duty to ensure employees’ safety, health and welfare at work as far as reasonably
practicable.
All contractors on site will be required to follow this legislation with their employees.
Management will ensure that there is provision for:
● Welfare facilities for all employees on site including break out area, designated
sanitary facilities.
● Provide and maintain a safe workplace which uses safe plant and equipment
● Prevent risks from use of any article or substance and from exposure to physical
agents, noise and vibration.
● Prevent any improper conduct or behaviour likely to put the safety, health and
welfare of employees at risk
● Provide instruction and training to employees on health and safety on site.
● Providing protective clothing and equipment to employees were required.
Employees on site will be expected to follow Section 13 of the act. This includes:
● Taking reasonable care to protect the health and safety of themselves and of other
people on the event site.
● Not to engage in improper behaviour that will endanger themselves or others
● Not to be under the influence of drink or drugs in the workplace
● To report any defects in the place of work or equipment which might be a danger
to health and safety.
● To report any incidents during the build phase and during event live with the
management team.
ACCESSIBILITY
ACCESS TO PARADE
A designated disability wheelchair area has to be identified yet .
PARKING
There will be normal on-street parking outside of the parade route. The organisers would
encourage the use of public transport.

Guide or Assistance dogs will be permitted to the event.
SUPPORT
Stewards and Volunteers will be briefed prior to the event on methods of support for
people on site and facilities available.
COMMUNITY GAIN
It is anticipated that this event will have a positive impact on the local community and as
such will result in new and returning patrons to the event.

TRANSFER OF PRIMACY
STATEMENT OF INTENT
The Event Management Team, comprising the Event Controller and Safety Officer as
appointed by the organiser’s for this event is responsible for the safe running and
management of this event and the crowd.
Transfer of Primacy
An Garda Síochána is responsible for the monitoring and addressing of Public Order
issues as well as the implementation, in the agreed manner, of the Traffic Management
Plan. Both parties will work together to ensure a safe and enjoyable event.
In the event of the implementation of the Emergency Plan, as agreed between the Event
Management Team and An Garda Síochána, and in consultation with the attending
medical services, An Garda Síochána and the Emergency Services will take primacy at
the event and the Event management Team and its agents will put themselves at the
disposal of the Emergency Services to implement the Emergency Plan.
In the event of the Emergency Plan being successfully implemented and the event
subsequently being safe to re-proceed, Ań Garda Síochána and the Emergency Services
shall return primacy and overall control of the event to the Event Management Team.
All such decisions and changes of primacy shall be recorded, in writing, and signed off
on by all parties in the Event Log.
Facility is also in place for partial transfer of primacy in the event that the Emergency
Plan is to be implemented in a specific, localised area where it is not affecting the
event in its entirety at that time. Again, this will be detailed and recorded in writing
in the Event Log, as outlined above.
SIGNED: DATED:
Ań Garda Síochána / Fire Officer
Event Controller
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
Procedure for CPPP The Whale Street on the narrow streets of Galway.
Having seen The Whale Street to mitigate the risk to the public this procedure is to be
applied to move the float safely through the streets of Galway.
There is a team of five who will be holding a rope forming the shape of a V and stretching
this rope to the Preestablished measurements to create a space wide enough for the
float plus 1.2 metres between the float and the nearest member of audience.
This team will move ahead of the float establish the route, will then be followed by
Stewarts who will reinforce the route.
Alongside the float will be 8 people each side holding a rope and creating a cordon
around the performance, this cordon is to be kept in place from the beginning of the
parade till it reaches the end at the Spanish Arch.
Prior to the event areas that would be concerned to event control will have markings
placed on the road to assist Security and area managers to maximise the available
space for the float to pass safely.
In consideration of the size of the The Whale Street and it’s ability to expand and
height, Event control area manager who will be ahead of the parade will be monitoring
the wind speed with a handheld anemometer. The company has advised that the The
Whale Streetcan operate in gusts up to 60 KM per hour.
Street furniture
There is a number of premises along the route with street furniture that need to be
removed prior to the event. Contact from the event organisers and licensing authority in
writing plus a site visit is required to ensure route is clear.
CCTV Plan
Event control has 8 wireless cameras that will be fitted along the route. These cameras
have the ability to rotate and maximise the viewing area. The location of each camera
will be determined by what’s available to mount them to and plan will be formed when
cameras are deployed.
Hostile vehicle mitigation
Map to be DRAWN

SCHEDULE
The following is a schedule for CPPP The Whale Street Spectacle 2026
16th July. company assembly at Nimmo’s Pier.
17th July. Volunteers and Security briefing with rehearsal @7.30pm
Volunteers and Security get into there assigned positions @ 9.00pm
Company makes their way from Nimmo’s Pier under escort along Merchants
Road
to reach the pedestrian street and complete their preparations at the public
toilets.
Performance 1begins @ 9.30pm
Performance ends at Spanish Arch @10.45pm
The Whale Streetreturns to Nimmo’s Pier.
18th July.Volunteers and security returns for briefing @ 4.30pm
Company leaves Nimmo’s and return to Eyre Square via Merchants Road for
5.50pm to complete set up.
Performance 2 begins @9.30pm.
Spectacle ends at Spanish Arch @10.45pm
Company returns to Nimmo’s Pier for derig.

Section 1
1.1 Introduction and Objectives
Galway International Arts Festival are organizing a street spectacle throughout the
streets of Galway on 17th and 18th of July 2026. It is a large Whale structure. beginning
at Eyre Square on to Shop street ,Maingaurd Street, Bridge Street,Dominick Street ,and
concluding at the Spanish Arch.
Event Control Centre is located in The Galway Advertiser’s offices Eyre Square.
The main objective of the GIAF and indeed the main objective of the Medical Plan will be to:
• Ensure that required Medical Treatment is available and administered to persons attending the
event as required
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 68of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
• Reduce as far as practicable or eliminate the impact on HSE services. Reducing the impact on the
existing services will help to ensure that the normal levels of care can be provided to the general
community in Galway City and its surrounds.
The objectives will be achieved by the coordination of the various Agencies and
Organizations involved and will include the HSE, Fire Brigade, Civil Defense, Galway City
Council and
Ań Garda Siochana the coordination will be achieved by meetings and various
communications between all parties.
The scope of this plan relates only to GIAF street spectacle on 17th and 18th of July 2026.
1.2 References
• Code of Practice for Safety at Outdoor Pop Concerts and other Outdoor Musical
Events

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
2.4 Control of Noise
We have considered the effect of noise when selecting the location of the Medical facilities and do not
envisage that the noise levels to be intrusive.
2.5 Duties and Responsibilities
Medical Coordinator Duties
• Act as the single point of contact for the Medical Plan.
• Participate in Medical/Event planning meetings and sign off on Event Medical Plan and any drills and
rehearsals
• Ensure all medical personnel are briefed on the event medical plan
• To provide overall coordination of the various medical services providing cover at the event.
• To act as point of contact for the HSE Environmental Health Service
• To liaise with the Event Organisers, Event Controller, Event Safety Officer, the National Ambulance
Service Control, the Voluntary Emergency Services, An Garda Síochána, the HSE Environmental
Health Service and any other relevant agencies/personnel.
• To allocate medical resources effectively prior to and during the event and to keep a register of all
those who have roles in the event medical plan.
• To ensure that all relevant process and contact details are in place for effective and efficient
communications.
• To ensure that the appropriate PPE has been provided.
• To establish and maintain links throughout the event as appropriate with the receiving hospitals and
the HSE emergency services
• To ensure that the Patient Care Report forms are completed for all medical assists and provide a
summary to the event organizers post event
• To attend end of event debrief
• Ensure command structure is established.
2.5.1 Deputy Medical Coordinator Duties
The Deputy Medical Coordinator duties will be as follows:
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 71of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
• To assume the duties of the Event Medical Coordinator should the Event Medical Coordinator not
be in a position to carry out his duties
• To actively support the Event Medical Coordinator in his role
2.6 Event Control/Operational Timing
The operational timings of Event Control and Medical Control will be as follows:
Date Operational Timing Comments
17/7/26, 18/7/26
8.30pm til 11pm
2.7Medical / First Aid Procedures
The Civil Defense will provide first aid cover to the event.
First Aid Posts Procedures
All event medical personnel will operate within their scope of practice, as defined by Irish Medical
Council (doctors), An Bord Altranais (nurses) and Pre-Hospital Emergency Care Council (Advanced
Paramedics, Paramedics, EMT’s and Emergency First Responders). The Event Medical Coordinator
retains overall clinical responsibility for patients who seek medical/first-aid assistance at the event. As
such, all patient treatment / transfer requests will be notified to the Event Medical Coordinator for
approval.
The following procedures apply to first aid posts:
• Crews manning a First Aid Post must report to Medical Control once post is operational.
• The post should be manned by agreed complement at all times.
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 72of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
• Procedure for treatment of patients in the First Aid Post should follow the Patient Management
Procedures detailed in this document
• Where there is doubt about a patient’s condition a member of the Post crew must contact medical
control/ doctor for their advice and direction on the situation.
• With all suspected medical conditions, a doctor must be consulted.
• No medication of any type is to be administered to a patient at a First Aid Post without the direction of
a Practitioner.
• All relevant patient and treatment details must be recorded on the relevant form and returned to
Medical Control at the end of each day.
• On completion of the day’s duty the person in charge of the post must ensure that:
a) The post is secure.
b) All relevant forms are handed in to the Medical Coordinator.
c) All communications equipment is handed back to Medical Control.
First Aid Posts will only be closed down on the direction of Medical Control
Provide first aid staff & ambulances as agreed within their patient care protocols
- Operate within their level of competence
- Be identifiable in high visibility/reflective uniform
- Be 18 years and over.
- Report any concerns in respect to audience safety to the Event Safety Officer via their senior
officer/Medical Control.
- Be present on site at the agreed start time and remain on site until such time as medical/First Aid
operations are stood down
- To transport patients both on and off site as directed
- Perform their duties in accordance with their level of training.
- Complete all documentation required in relation to patient care.
- Will direct all media enquiries to the relevant PRO/Media personnel.
The following indicates the patient management procedure to be adhered to during the event:
Level 1
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 73of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
Patients who present to the onsite First Aid Posts will be initially assessed by the first aid staff at this
facility. Following this quick assessment and noting of the suspected condition the patient will either be
administered first aid treatment and discharged from the facility or referred to the next available level of
care available. At this stage the patient’s details must be recorded on the approved PHECC (Pre Hospital
Emergency Care Council) patient report form. If patient is encountered in the field away from a First Aid
Post and further treatment is required the patient must be transported to the nearest First Aid Post. If the
patient is unable to undertake this journey due to the nature of their condition then further assistance must
be requested via medical control.
Level 2
Patients at First Aid Posts who require further assistance outside the scope of first aid should be referred
to the Emergency Medical Technicians (EMTs) at the post who will asses the patient further and administer
treatment as required prior to discharge. Emergency Medical Technicians may also encounter patients in
the field and will adhere to the procedures laid down in level one for the management of patients.
Level 3
Patients requiring further assessment and/or treatment off site will be referred to an appropriate medical
facility. Contact must be made via the Control with the proposed receiving hospital given the details of the
intended transfer. No patient should be sent to a medical facility off site without first going through Medical
Control.
Documentation
All medical and first aid providers will provide a report for each patient. This will be completed on the
PHECC (Pre Hospital Emergency Care Council) patient report form; the form should be filled in as
applicable with all available details recorded. The patient form is transported with the patient to be issued
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 74of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
to HSE staff at the applicable Hospital should the patient require additional care. The copy of the form
(there is one copy in addition to the original) is held by the first aid organization acting on behalf of the event
organiser.
The PHECC (Pre Hospital Emergency Care Council) patient report form will be completed as appropriate
to each case for all enquires to First Aid Posts.
Patient Discharge
• Patients may be discharged back to the site if their complaint is minor (e.g. Simple wound,
uncomplicated headache, sore throat).
• Patients who require further assessment, (e.g. ECG) will be referred to the hospital in addition to those
clearly requiring admission.
• The PHECC (Pre Hospital Emergency Care Council) patient report form incorporates a discharge
section and will be used in all cases.
2.8 Referrals to Hospital
All patients who require further treatment at hospital should be sent to the Emergency Department,
University Hospital Galway with the completed PHECC (Pre Hospital Emergency Care Council) patient
report form;
No patient will be sent to hospital from the event without having been assessed first except in exceptional
circumstances, by an authorised professional. The Emergency Department of the receiving hospital and
NAS National Emergency Operations Centre will be notified of all such transfers
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins

Section 3
3.1 Event Medical Communications
Event medical radio communications will be managed by Event Medical Co-ordinator and Event Control
based at Event Control Office. Event Medical communications shall also have direct radio contact with the
following persons:
• Event Controller
• An Garda Síochána (Senior Guard on duty)
• Safety Officer
• Chief Steward
Communications will be also be available via landline and mobile phones; additional capacity may be
provided by the network providers with designated key mobile telephone numbers to be notified to the
mobile network providers to ensure coverage is prioritized where they are used.
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 76of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
Calls will be handled in a number of ways depending on the type and what is required.
Medical control: where all communications will be handled for movement of ambulances and personnel,
handling of incidents, and all medical issues.
Calls for assistance may be received in a number of ways
➢ By telephone (landline)
➢ By mobile phone
➢ By radio
➢ Running call by medical personnel already close to the incident.
➢ From NAS National Emergency Operations Centre
➢ From Garda control
All information will be logged.
The following details to be confirmed:
➢ L Location Where exactly are you?
➢ I Incident What has happened?
➢ R Requirement What exactly do you need?
➢ A Acknowledge Were you heard?
All callers to clearly identify themselves.
All incoming incident calls will be prioritized should there be a large number of calls received at once.
Medical control will prioritize according to the severity of injury, the appropriate level of response will then
be sent to each incident, and calls will be dispatched via the medical control radio network.
Call Signs
Call signs will be allocated at the commencement of each day.
A chronological login hard copy of all activity to medical control will be maintained.
The following is a sample of how medical communications would operate at the event.

In the case of 112/999 calls being made by a member of the public at the event site the following will apply:
• These calls will be taken by National Ambulance Service, as per normal procedures. All calls will
receive an appropriate response and a HSE Emergency Ambulance will be dispatched to the
scene, where appropriate. NAS may contact the Event Medical Control on the dedicated number
087 7387333 to notify the Event Medical Coordinator of the call and/or to seek assistance from

access route is kept clear in so far as possible for the approaching ambulance.
