3.6 Sirens and lights
Sirens and lights will be used only as required under the direction of the Medical Coordinator, Medical
Control will liaise with the Event Controller and Gardai to ensure that the required assistance is provided
to ensure clear access and egress to and to from the scene. Adherence to ‘The use of blue lights and sirens’
legislation and the ’Road Traffic Act’ will be complied with at all times
Section 4
Please refer to other relevant sections of the Event Plan for details of overall Event management and fire
safety procedures
4.1 Ambulance Emergency Route
The emergency route is as indicated within the event traffic management plan, See maps
4.2 Procedures for Fire/Security Incidents
These are covered in main plan.
Section 5
5.1 Infection Prevention and Control
Standard precautions will apply in order to prevent infection cross between first aid/medical personnel
and patients; this will include use of appropriate PPE.
5.2 Clinical Waste
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 80of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
All clinical waste generated from first aid activities will be subject to appropriate storage and disposal. This
will relate to sharps, dressings and personal protective equipment. The clinical waste will be securely
disposed of by the provider of Medical services of the Civil Defense.
5.3 Environmental Health
The organizers will ensure that the appropriate arrangements are in place at the event to ensure the health
of performers, patrons and the general public.
This will include the following:
• Appropriate sanitary arrangements will be provided in all areas in accordance with Code of
Practice for Safety at Outdoor Pop Concerts and other Outdoor Musical Events. (Appropriate
maintenance and servicing will be maintained)
• Water
• Appropriate collections of waste generated from the event.
A full schedule of casual traders particularly food vendors will be provided to the HSE EHO prior to the
event.
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 81of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
Section 6
The HSE Emergency Management office will forward a copy of the final draft of this plan to
the HSE National Ambulance Service for comment & observations. The Final Medical plan
will be forwarded to NAS National Emergency Operations Centre in advance of the event
Section 7 Major Emergency Plan
SERIOUS INCIDENT
A serious incident may be declared by the Event Medical Coordinator when the
resources available on-site via the event medical team are unable to deal with the
severity or number of attendees requiring medical assistance. This may require
assistance from the National Ambulance Service in terms of personnel, ambulances or
other equipment. The decision to call upon these resources will be made by the Event
Medical Coordinator via the NAS National Emergency Operations Centre OR the NAS
Liaison Officer if in attendance.
The Request will be made using using the ETHANE format:
E - the Exact location
T - the Type of incident
H - the Hazards present and potential
A - the Access to the location of the incident and egress route
N - the Number and severity of casualties
E - the Emergency services present and required at the scene
7.1 Major Emergency
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 82of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
Definition of a Major Emergency
“A Major Emergency is any event which, usually with little or no warning, causes
or threatens to cause death or injury, serious disruption of essential services, or
damage to property or the environment or infrastructure beyond the normal
capabilities of the principal emergency services in the area in which the event
occurs and requires the activation of specific additional procedures and the
mobilization of additional resources to ensure an effective, coordinated
response.”(A Framework for Major Emergency Management 2006)
A Major Emergency can only be declared by an authorized officer of one of the Principal Response
Agencies (PRA’s)
In the event of a major emergency, the Controller of Operations of the PRA’s Event Controller will
proceed immediately to Event Control (If appropriate) . This area may be designated the On-Site
Coordination Centre.
7.1 Emergency Procedures
ACTION UPON DISCOVERY OF FIRE
Outdoor
• Person discovering the Fire to immediately inform Event Controller.
• The Event Controller will contact the emergency services required directly. They will state the
location and source (if known) of the fire.
• The Event Controller will contact the Medical Coordinator
• Stewarding/Fire Personnel to attack the fire if it is safe to do so if appropriate equipment is
available.
• In the case of a serious fire a decision will be made to evacuate the area as required.
Indoor (Venues)
• Raise alarm both verbally and by nearest MCP (Manual Call Point/Break Glass)
• Fight fire only if safe to do so
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 83of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
• Evacuate the building using the nearest emergency route
• Proceed to assembly point
• Wait at assembly point until directed otherwise by an authorized person only (Authorized person
will be your fire warden)
EMERGENCY/SECURITY PROCEDURES IN THE EVENT OF BOMB THREAT/SUSPECT PACKAGE
• The Event Controller to be immediately informed of all details in the event of bomb threat/suspect
package.
• The situation to be assessed by the Event Controller and senior Garda Officer present based on
the information received in order to determine the veracity of the threat.
• If a decision is made to carry out a search this will be carried out using the most competent
persons available. If a package is found the area is to be secured and the Event Controller is to be
informed.
• The Senior Garda officer present will take the decision to evacuate the area is applicable.
PROCEDURES IN THE EVENT OF CROWD DISTURBANCE/OVERCROWDING
• The senior steward on duty to inform Event Control to enable additional resources to be provided
if required.
• The GARDAI in association with the Safety Officer will initiate procedures to resolve the situation.
This may include:
➢ Cessation of Music or activities either immediately or in a phased controlled way.
➢ Structured announcements to the crowd advising as to procedures, i.e. directions to
move away etc.
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
EVACUATION PROCEDURES
If the evacuation is due to serious fire, bomb, bomb threat or other emergency the following are the
procedures. This relates mainly to outdoor events. This will be jointly organized and managed by the
Gardai, Safety Officer and Senior Stewards present. Other principal response agencies will be
incorporated as applicable.
• Production personnel/persons co-ordinating the activities to be advised so that activities can be
suspended.
• Appropriate communications to be directed to the public by PA systems or at local areas
dependent on scale of the evacuation.
• Stewards to assist the Gardai in manning the evacuation and ensuring that evacuation routes are
kept clear in so far as possible.
• All persons engaged in the management of the drill procedure to act in a calm organized manner.
• In the event of a major incident the Gardai will assume ultimate control.
• Consideration will be made to arranging temporary large scale assembly areas, these areas will
where possible offer basic levels of welfare.
Assembly Points/Areas
Designation of assembly points where evacuees can safely congregate in the event of an emergency will
be developed.
Larger assembly areas/zones will be established for in the event of a large scale evacuation in terms of
outdoor activities. An example of this could be the evacuation of numerous persons from a particular area
of the town.
The availability of potable water/shelter and welfare facilities are important in cases where large groupings
of people are held in an area for a period of time. It is also possible that evacuees would return to their cars
in this scenario although this may lead to congestion of approach roads which in turn could delay
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 85of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
emergency services coming from authorities outside the town. Where the potential for this occurs it may
be more prudent to restrict persons returning to their vehicles for a period of time
Park and ride facilities could be used to transfer persons with reduced personal mobility from areas of risk.
In all cases of evacuation the utmost priority will be afforded to ensuring that assembly points and areas
offer the required protection and welfare to the persons affected
Section 8 (Miscellaneous)
8.1 Crowd Densities
As stated in the overall Event Draft Plan, the capacities for particular areas will be determined at 30,000.
Crowd capacities will not be exceeded; this will be managed by stewarding, personnel counter systems
and barriers. Active monitoring will be provided by Safety officer and Security personnel during
performances.
8.2 Unaccompanied Persons/Lost Children
Please liaise with member of Ań Garda Siochana in the event of. The review stand will act as central
meeting point for lost children.
CPPP The Whale Street 2026 Draft Issue Date: 16-4-26 Controller: F
Commins
Page 86of 91

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
Section 9 Risk Assessment for River.
The parade is crossing the river on two location at O Brian’s bridge and Wolf tone bridge.
O Brian’sBridge will have six persons supervising the bridge to encourage people to not sit on the wall,
I will also have Claddagh Watch personnel with throw lines monitoring the river.
It is worth noting that the tides for these parade timing is for tides to be going out or gone out and the time
of the year water levels would be also low.
I will be made contact with Shane Coogan,Water safety officer for Galway City and County.
This is the proposal at the time of writing this draft but we will have to take into consideration weather
conditions in the lead up to the event that with a spell of poor weather water level could be higher then
seasonal expected and assessment will be made on dealing with safety concerns on days before the
event.

CROWD SAFETY AND MOVEMENT
EXPECTED NUMBERS
This is a free event
doc_risks.description:
CROWD SAFETY AND MOVEMENT
EXPECTED NUMBERS
This is a free event. The numbers expected to attend will be approximately 10,000, based
on previous year attendance but also very weather dependent if a wet day the numbers
will be less.
CAPACITY CALCULATIONS
A review of the event-specific capacity calculations will be carried out and will continue
to be reviewed as any amendments or additions occur. These calculations consider all
the relevant physical (P) and safety (S) factors, and include:
● Ingres
controls:
When calculating, consideration
has been given to items such as fixed structures in situ and space given to
event elements for example barriers and sterile area, as well as exit routes

● Egress Capacity which relates to the time required for the maximum number
of attendees to exit the event through the exit points

● Emergency egress that is concerned with the egress capacity in the case of an
emergency and ensuring there are sufficient routes for the expected
occupants (participants, spectators and personnel)

When reviewing the capacities for the event, various areas and matters are considered
and these included:
● Main Entry/Exits
● Emergency routes
It has been established that the venue is a suitable site for the proposed activity

emergency route from the performance route

occupancy

controls:
CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
Any member of event control staff of the three principal response agencies will inform
the Event Controller

The event controller will inform the Stewards in the relevant area to:
Implement the appropriate crowd diversion plan for that area

Once the access street
has been closed except to exiting audience, all crowds will be diverted to areas
specified by the controller

The designated area Head Steward will be instructed to
make the appropriate emergency evacuation announcements

Incident: Person Falling In River
Preventative measures will be in place.These measures include a number of stewards
backed up with members of Claddagh Watch will be briefed accordingly where they will
advise the public not to sit on the bridge wall

Persons will be rescued in accordance with the standard emergency procedures for
water rescue

Incident: Infiltration of route or Reviewing stand by Uninvited Groups
The Area supervisor or Area Garda officer will immediately inform the Event controller
identifying the extent and the location of the incident

The event controller, Senior Garda office in charge will issue the appropriate orders to
Garda officers/stewards in the relevant area to:
Implement the appropriate crowd diversion plan for that area/Reviewing stand

Once
the access street/Reviewing stand has been closed except to exiting audience, all
crowds will be diverted to areas specified by the controller and senior Garda Officer

The designated area Head Steward will be instructed to make the appropriate
emergency evacuation announcements

controls:
CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
Emergency Plan is implemented due to an incident occurring, the event management
team will no longer manage the event

The Event Controller will consult with the Principle Response Agency (PRA) advising them
of the situation, and the PRA’s will coordinate an effective response

TRANSFER OF PRIMACY
Transfer of responsibility from the Event Management Team to an Emergency Controller,
and transfer back, will be logged in the Event Log and will also involve the signing of the
Transfer of Primacy form

EMERGENCY CONTROLLER
The Emergency Controller shall take control over the entire operation unless or until the
circumstances of the emergency dictate otherwise, i.e., the Senior Fire Officer present
would take a major fire in charge

In this event the Emergency Controller would be
responsible for keeping the emergency area clear

LOCATION OF EMERGENCY
The location of the actual emergency must be clearly identified by the Emergency
Controller before deciding which options in the emergency plan are to be activated

DIVERSION
If the Emergency Controller considers that a full (or at least substantial) diversion is
required, he/she will inform the Event Management Team, who will assist with the
management of the diversion

All patrons will be directed away from the emergency towards
the nearest emergency route, which may be manned

EMERGENCY ACCESS ROUTE
All relevant emergency services will be made aware of the emergency routes in advance
of the event
This happens as part of the event management planning process

CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
Under Section 8 of the Safety, Health and Welfare at Work Act 2005 the employer has a
duty to ensure employees’ safety, health and welfare at work as far as reasonably
practicable

Management will ensure that there is provision for:
● Welfare facilities for all employees on site including break out area, designated
sanitary facilities

● Provide and maintain a safe workplace which uses safe plant and equipment
● Prevent risks from use of any article or substance and from exposure to physical
agents, noise and vibration

● Prevent any improper conduct or behaviour likely to put the safety, health and
welfare of employees at risk
● Provide instruction and training to employees on health and safety on site

ACCESSIBILITY
ACCESS TO PARADE
A designated disability wheelchair area has to be identified yet

PARKING
There will be normal on-street parking outside of the parade route

doc_risks.title:
TRANSFER OF PRIMACY
STATEMENT OF INTENT
The Event Management Team, comprising the Event Controller and Safety Officer as
doc_risks.description:
TRANSFER OF PRIMACY
STATEMENT OF INTENT
The Event Management Team, comprising the Event Controller and Safety Officer as
appointed by the organiser’s for this event is responsible for the safe running and
management of this event and the crowd.
Transfer of Primacy
An Garda Síochána is responsible for the monitoring and addressing of Public Order
issues as well as the implementation, in the agreed manner, of the Traffic Management
Plan. Both parties will work together to ensure a safe and enjoyab
controls:
TRANSFER OF PRIMACY
STATEMENT OF INTENT
The Event Management Team, comprising the Event Controller and Safety Officer as
appointed by the organiser’s for this event is responsible for the safe running and
management of this event and the crowd

Transfer of Primacy
An Garda Síochána is responsible for the monitoring and addressing of Public Order
issues as well as the implementation, in the agreed manner, of the Traffic Management
Plan

In the event of the Emergency Plan being successfully implemented and the event
subsequently being safe to re-proceed, Ań Garda Síochána and the Emergency Services
shall return primacy and overall control of the event to the Event Management Team

All such decisions and changes of primacy shall be recorded, in writing, and signed off
on by all parties in the Event Log

Facility is also in place for partial transfer of primacy in the event that the Emergency
Plan is to be implemented in a specific, localised area where it is not affecting the
event in its entirety at that time

SIGNED: DATED:
Ań Garda Síochána / Fire Officer
Event Controller

controls:
CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
Procedure for CPPP The Whale Street on the narrow streets of Galway

Having seen The Whale Street to mitigate the risk to the public this procedure is to be
applied to move the float safely through the streets of Galway

There is a team of five who will be holding a rope forming the shape of a V and stretching
this rope to the Preestablished measurements to create a space wide enough for the
float plus 1.2 metres between the float and the nearest member of audience

This team will move ahead of the float establish the route, will then be followed by
Stewarts who will reinforce the route

Prior to the event areas that would be concerned to event control will have markings
placed on the road to assist Security and area managers to maximise the available
space for the float to pass safely

In consideration of the size of the The Whale Street and it’s ability to expand and
height, Event control area manager who will be ahead of the parade will be monitoring
the wind speed with a handheld anemometer

Street furniture
There is a number of premises along the route with street furniture that need to be
removed prior to the event

Contact from the event organisers and licensing authority in
writing plus a site visit is required to ensure route is clear

CCTV Plan
Event control has 8 wireless cameras that will be fitted along the route

These cameras
have the ability to rotate and maximise the viewing area

• Act as the single point of contact for the Medical Plan

rehearsals
• Ensure all medical personnel are briefed on the event medical plan
• To provide overall coordination of the various medical services providing cover at the event

• To allocate medical resources effectively prior to and during the event and to keep a register of all
those who have roles in the event medical plan

• To ensure that all relevant process and contact details are in place for effective and efficient
communications

doc_risks.description:
CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
• Procedure for treatment of patients in the First Aid Post should follow the Patient Management
Procedures detailed in this document
• Where there is doubt about a patient’s condition a member of the Post crew must contact medical
control/ doctor for their advice and direction on the situation.
• With all suspected medical conditions, a doctor must be consulted.
• No medication of any type is to be administered to a patient at a First Aid
controls:
• No medication of any type is to be administered to a patient at a First Aid Post without the direction of
a Practitioner

• All relevant patient and treatment details must be recorded on the relevant form and returned to
Medical Control at the end of each day
c) All communications equipment is handed back to Medical Control

First Aid Posts will only be closed down on the direction of Medical Control
Provide first aid staff & ambulances as agreed within their patient care protocols
- Operate within their level of competence
- Be identifiable in high visibility/reflective uniform
- Be 18 years and over

- Report any concerns in respect to audience safety to the Event Safety Officer via their senior
officer/Medical Control

- Be present on site at the agreed start time and remain on site until such time as medical/First Aid
operations are stood down
- To transport patients both on and off site as directed
- Perform their duties in accordance with their level of training

controls:
CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
Patients who present to the onsite First Aid Posts will be initially assessed by the first aid staff at this
facility

Following this quick assessment and noting of the suspected condition the patient will either be
administered first aid treatment and discharged from the facility or referred to the next available level of
care available

If patient is encountered in the field away from a First Aid
Post and further treatment is required the patient must be transported to the nearest First Aid Post

If the
patient is unable to undertake this journey due to the nature of their condition then further assistance must
be requested via medical control

Level 2
Patients at First Aid Posts who require further assistance outside the scope of first aid should be referred
to the Emergency Medical Technicians (EMTs) at the post who will asses the patient further and administer
treatment as required prior to discharge

Emergency Medical Technicians may also encounter patients in
the field and will adhere to the procedures laid down in level one for the management of patients

Contact must be made via the Control with the proposed receiving hospital given the details of the
intended transfer

No patient should be sent to a medical facility off site without first going through Medical
Control

Documentation
All medical and first aid providers will provide a report for each patient

The patient form is transported with the patient to be issued

3.2 112/999 Calls from the Public at the Event.
In the case of 112/999 calls being made by a member of the public at the event site the following will apply:
• These calls will be taken by National Ambulance Service, as per normal procedures. All calls will

doc_risks.description:

Definition of a Major Emergency
“A Major Emergency is any event which, usually with little or no warning, causes
or threatens to cause death or injury, serious disruption of essential services, or
damage to property or the environment or infrastructure beyond the normal
capabilities of the principal emergency services in the area in which the event
occurs and requires the activation of specific additional procedures and the
mobilization of
controls:
This area may be designated the On-Site
Coordination Centre

7.1 Emergency Procedures
ACTION UPON DISCOVERY OF FIRE
Outdoor
• Person discovering the Fire to immediately inform Event Controller
• The Event Controller will contact the emergency services required directly

• The Event Controller will contact the Medical Coordinator
• Stewarding/Fire Personnel to attack the fire if it is safe to do so if appropriate equipment is
available
• In the case of a serious fire a decision will be made to evacuate the area as required

Indoor (Venues)
• Raise alarm both verbally and by nearest MCP (Manual Call Point/Break Glass)
• Fight fire only if safe to do so

EVACUATION PROCEDURES
If the evacuation is due to serious fire, bomb, bomb threat or other emergency the following are the
procedures. This relates mainly to outdoor events. This will be jointly organized and managed by the
Gardai, Safety Officer and Senior Stewards present. Other principal response agencies will be
incorporated as applicable.
• Production personnel/persons co-ordinating the activities to be advised so that activities can b
controls:
CPPP The Whale Street GIAF 2026 | EVENT MANAGEMENT PLAN
EVACUATION PROCEDURES
If the evacuation is due to serious fire, bomb, bomb threat or other emergency the following are the
procedures

This will be jointly organized and managed by the
Gardai, Safety Officer and Senior Stewards present

• Appropriate communications to be directed to the public by PA systems or at local areas
dependent on scale of the evacuation

• Stewards to assist the Gardai in manning the evacuation and ensuring that evacuation routes are
kept clear in so far as possible
• All persons engaged in the management of the drill procedure to act in a calm organized manner
• In the event of a major incident the Gardai will assume ultimate control

• Consideration will be made to arranging temporary large scale assembly areas, these areas will
where possible offer basic levels of welfare

Assembly Points/Areas
Designation of assembly points where evacuees can safely congregate in the event of an emergency will
be developed

Larger assembly areas/zones will be established for in the event of a large scale evacuation in terms of
outdoor activities

An example of this could be the evacuation of numerous persons from a particular area
of the town
